content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN); node 111 is the root. Each node has a value; let's denote the value of node iii by AiA_iAi‚Äč.

You may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero): choose any node which still exists in the tree and remove the whole subtree of this node including itself.

Let's define the profit as the sum of values of all nodes which currently exist in the tree minus X‚čÖkX \cdot kX‚čÖk, where kkk denotes the number of times this operation was performed. Find the maximum possible profit.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
Each of the following N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the maximum profit.
